english: or kyogs (khyogs) palanquin, sedan-chair, litter Pth.; k̥yogs-dpyáń ('khyogs dpyang) id.; a lath or pole for carrying burdens Sch.
pf. and imp. kyoń (khyon) to bring W.

pf. k̥yoms ('khyoms) 1. to rock, to wave, of a ship Schr., of the water Sch. (not quite clear); *kyom-kyom do-ba* (*khyom khyom do ba*) C. to reel, stagger, *čáń-ghi k̥yom-pa dug* (*chang ghi 'khyom pa dug*) he is staggering under the influence of beer; to be dizzy Med.; mtso-k̥yóm (mtsho 'khyom) dizziness, vertigo, ni. f.; lug-glád mgo-k̥ór k̥yóm-pa ɣso (lug glad mgo 'khor 'khyom pa gso) the brain of a sheep cures the swimming of the head (vertigo) Med.
1. to miss, fail, not to hit Cs. -- 2. to reel, stagger, from intoxication. -- 3. to warp, of wood.

pf. k̥yol ('khyol), cf. skyél-ba (skyel ba), to be carried, to be brought (somewhere) Pth.; with ɣnód-pa (gnod pa) to be done, inflicted Mil.; to arrive at, come to, reach, sku-tsé mtá-ru (sku tshe mtha' ru) the end of life.
Sch., k̥yós-ma ('khyos ma) Mil., a present, gift, = kyós ma (khyos ma), skyás-ma (skyas ma).

I. vb., pf. prob. k̥ras ('khras) to lean to, to incline towards Cs.; k̥rá-sa ('khra sa) a support to lean against, a prop, back (of a chair) Lex. -- II. adj. hard, = k̥ráń-ba ('khrang ba), mkráń-ba (mkhrang ba) Sch.

pf. bkrab (?) (bkrab), cf. also skráb-pa (skrab pa), 1. to strike, to beat, in repeated strokes, as in swimming and rowing; to thrust, stamp, thump, tread heavily, bro k̥ráb-pa (bro 'khrab pa) to dance in that manner Mil., Pth. -- 2. to winnow, to fan Stg., col. *táb-pa* (*thab pa*). -- 3. *mīy ṭab ṭab* (*mig 'khrab 'khrab*) (or *ṭab-ṭab* (*'khrab 'khrab*)) *jè'-pa* (*byed pa*) C., *čò-če* (*co ce*) W., to blink, twinkle, wink with the eyes. -- 4. *ḱa-šàg ṭab-čè* (*kha shag 'khrab ce*) W. to jest, to joke, to crack jokes. -- 5. Sch.: to leap, jump, Schr. for joy. -- 6. to scoop out, to bail out Sch. -- 7. to fight, to combat C., W.

pf. k̥ris ('khris), cf. dkri-ba (dkri ba), cognate to k̥ril-ba ('khril ba), 1. to wind, roll; twist one's self, to coil (of snakes) Dzl.; kyim-táb-kyi k̥ri-ba (khyim thab kyi 'khri ba) conjugal embrace Pth.; *o̥g-ma ṭi-se* (*'og ma 'khri se*) (for k̥ri-ste ('khri ste)) *rag* (rag) W. I have a sore throat, prop. I feel my throat tied up, I am choking; fig.: kún-la k̥ris-pa (kun la 'khris pa), either as an adj. 'ensnaring', or as a sbst. 'ensnarer' = sin, cf. kun-dkrís (kun dkris) in dkrī-ba (dkri ba); k̥rí-šiń ('khri shing) = k̥ril-šiń ('khril shing). -- 2. mostly as a sbst.: the being attached to, given to, c.c. genit. (synonym of čúgs-pa (chugs pa)): rań-dón-gyi (rang don gyi), to one's own advantage, bu-smád-kyi (bu smad kyi) to wife and children Mil.; fondness, attachment; żen-k̥rís (zhen 'khris) id. -- 3. kral k̥ri-ba (khral 'khri ba) to impose a tax C., Lex.

I. sbst. 1. (Ssk. मिथुन) coitus (of the two sexes), copulation, pairing, the usual, not exactly obscene, yet not euphemistic term for it; k̥rig-pa spyód-pa ('khrig pa spyod pa), also k̥rig-čágs spyód-pa ('khrig chags spyod pa) B. and C., *ṭig-pa čó-če* (*'khrig pa co ce*) W., to lie with etc.; k̥rig-pai čós-la rtén-pa ('khrig pa'i chos la rten pa) to indulge in lust, to be given to voluptuousness; k̥rig-skád ('khrig skad) Sch., k̥rig-tsíg ('khrig tshig) Lexx., obscene words, unchaste language; k̥rig-pa b̥yín-pa ('khrig pa 'byin pa) to talk smut. -- 2. a sign of the zodiac, the twins. -- 3. symb. num.: 2. II. vb. 1. to cohere, to stick together Cs. -- 2. to be clouded (of the sky), ɣnam k̥rig (gnam 'khrig) the sky is getting overcast; also *ṭigs soń* (*'khrigs song*) W. without a sbst., it has become cloudy, dull; o̥d-zér dań ǰa-o̥d k̥rig-pa ('od zer dang 'ja' 'od 'khrig pa) wrapt in rays of light and the splendours of the rainbow Pth.; tań tams-čád mes k̥rig-pa (thang thams cad mes 'khrig pa) the whole plain was enveloped in a flame of fire Mil. Cf. dkrígs-pa (dkrigs pa).

pf. k̥rid ('khrid), fut. bkri? (bkri), to lead, to conduct men or beasts to a place; to command, to head (an army); to bring along with, k̥rid-de ma o̥ńs-so ('khrid de ma 'ongs so) he has not brought (his wife) with him Dzl.; therefore k̥rid ('khrid) equivalent to 'with': bu-tsa k̥rid byuń-nas (bu tsha 'khrid byung nas) coming out with their children
